description: Cron scheduler priority engine validation — audit current cron cycles, priority ordering, and optimization opportunities
methodology: Cron cycle analysis across boredom scan, GRAO saturation detection, cycle logging requirements

activeCrons.name: GNW-Boredom-Scan
activeCrons.function: Boredom level calculation, stale item scoring, self-initiation

activeCrons.name: System-Health-Monitor
activeCrons.function: Gateway health, veto events, loop prevention

cronPatterns.workPattern: doc-refresh (81+ cycles) → pattern break → data analysis

findings.priorityOrdering: score-based (novelty × relevance × goal_alignment + 1.5x novelty boost for stale)
optimizationOpportunities: Priority engine: could add GRAO alignment weight (current tied to exp_04)
Frequency: boredom scan 30-min → could optimize to 45-min for idle system
Payload: cycle logs growing (144 logs, ~5KB each) → could implement log rotation
Stale threshold: 6h → could optimize to 12h for research docs (less critical)
Pattern break mechanism: manual detection → could implement auto-detection
currentLimitations: Priority ordering treats all docs equally — needs domain-weighted scoring
Stale threshold uniform (6h) — research docs less time-sensitive
Pattern break detection manual — could implement auto-detection
Cycle log size unbounded — needs rotation mechanism

validation.evidence: Cron scheduler operational with 144 cycles. Priority ordering score-based (novelty × relevance × goal_alignment). Pattern break mechanism working (cycles 131, 138, 139 breaking doc-refresh loops). Optimization opportunities: domain-weighted scoring, stale threshold per domain, log rotation, auto pattern detection.

notes: Cron scheduler is operational and healthy. GRAO alignment priority engine works (cycles 140, 143 tied to exp_04). Optimization: domain-weighted scoring, per-domain stale thresholds, log rotation, auto pattern detection.
